substep.title: Load Research Pattern Table
substep.action: Load references/codesmith/research-pattern-table.md into working memory. The question-shape quick-reference at the bottom maps shapes to patterns + child subagents.

substep.title: Split by Channel
substep.action:
For each question identified in step 2, classify:
- **User-directed**: only the user can answer (scope preferences, business constraints, deadlines, decisions between functional alternatives, foundational concerns).
- **Research agenda**: answerable by codebase/thoughts/web research — dispatch to researcher per the pattern table.
- **Artifact checks**: answerable by direct reads (the bundle itself, CONTEXT.md, briefs in thoughts/). No subagent needed.
Most turns will have AT MOST ONE user-directed question (per the per-turn user-checkpoint rule).

substep.title: Compose Dispatch Instructions (Hide-the-Intent)
substep.action:
For each research-agenda question, compose a dispatch entry:
- question_text: the focused question - pattern: from the pattern table (codebase-navigate, thoughts-navigate, architecture-zoom, prototype, diagnose-feedback-loop, external-research, escape-hatch) - child_subagent_hint: per the pattern (codebase-locator, codebase-analyzer, codebase-pattern-finder, thoughts-locator, thoughts-analyzer, web-search-researcher) — researcher honors the hint, and infers the child itself when it is omitted - scope: directory or file globs to constrain search - expected_output_shape: e.g., "markdown table with file:line and one-line purpose"
CRITICAL: the dispatch entry contains ONLY the above. NOT the ticket. NOT the parent decision context. NOT the design intent. researcher is blind to the change's intent. Its job is to gather facts about the codebase, not opinions about what to change.
Store the list as {{research_dispatch}}.

check.action:
Sharpen the user question: - One-sentence statement - RECOMMENDED answer FIRST in options (label ending "(Recommended)") - 2-4 alternatives with one-line trade-off descriptions - Never "I don't know, what do you think?" — the agent always has a position; the user redirects when the position is wrong.
Store as {{user_question}}. Asked in step 4a.

substep.title: Web Search Approval (if external-research in dispatch)

check.if: any dispatch entry uses external-research pattern
check.ask:
One research item this turn requires external/web research: {{topic}}.
Approve dispatching web-search-researcher? - Approve (Recommended): proceed with web search - Decline: drop the web item; rely on codebase + thoughts/ only

check.action: Remove the external-research entry from {{research_dispatch}}. Note in {{turn_notes}}: "web research deferred this turn."
